subject: #backend — OAuth token refresh debugging
summary: Ben and Maria debug the OAuth token refresh bug in Slack the afternoon before the formal bug triage. Maria shares the non-standard error payload and Ben agrees to escalate with the provider.

messages.bodyPreview: Seeing token refresh fail intermittently in staging. Provider is returning a plain-text 500 instead of the JSON error shape we coded against. Will share a capture.
Here's the raw body — not even valid JSON. Our error parser throws, then the retry loop never kicks in. So we lose the user's session.
Confirmed reproducing on my side. Let's file this as a P0 and raise a ticket with the provider today. Can you write up the repro for the triage tomorrow?
On it. Will link from the bug triage notes.

subject: Re: Onboarding design partner review — next steps
summary: Follow-up email thread with external design partner after the onboarding review. Priya sends written critique, Ben confirms the 3-screen direction, James picks up the action items.

messages.bodyPreview: Hi Ben — attached is the written critique from Tuesday's session. TL;DR: the 3-screen flow works, but screen 2 needs clearer CTA hierarchy and the progress indicator should be persistent, not only on the final step.
Thanks Priya — really useful. Looping James in. We're going to keep the 3-screen direction but pick up the CTA + progress indicator changes before the beta cohort expands.
Got it. I'll revise the CTA hierarchy and persistent progress bar by end of week. Will ping when ready for review.

subject: Launch timeline chat
summary: Ad-hoc WhatsApp group between Ben, James, and Lisa the evening after the stakeholder update. They sanity-check whether the auth blocker really does push the launch, and agree to push back publicly on the date only after the April 15 reassessment.

messages.bodyPreview: Quick one — after today's update I'm 80% sure we're slipping a week. Don't want to say it externally yet. Wait for the 15th reassessment?
Makes sense. As long as the beta cohort knows internally. Don't want surprises for the testers I onboarded.
Agreed. I'll hold off on updating the launch marketing assets until we have a confirmed date.
Perfect. Sticking with quiet reassessment for now. Thanks both.

subject: Beta feedback — week 2 usage notes
summary: External beta tester Mike sends a long-form email with attached screenshots detailing mobile rendering bugs. Sarah forwards to Alex for triage ahead of the beta debrief.

messages.bodyPreview: Hi team — summary of week 2 testing. Onboarding was smooth, but I've hit mobile layout issues on Android in both the dashboard and onboarding. Screenshots attached for each issue.
Thanks Mike — really helpful detail. Forwarding to Alex so she can fold this into the mobile layout work. We'll confirm fixes in the next beta build.
Got it. These map to the responsive regressions I'm already on. Will track against a5 and include Mike's screenshots in the PR description.

subject: #devops — deploy readiness check
summary: Slack thread where Tom asks for a readiness confirmation before enabling staging auto-promote. Maria flags the outstanding load test, Alex confirms frontend is green.

messages.bodyPreview: Planning to flip the staging-to-prod auto-promote toggle this afternoon. Anything I should hold for?
Load test at 1k users hasn't happened yet. I'd hold. I don't want the first prod push to be the first time we find a bottleneck.
Frontend is green — all checks pass. Happy either way but defer to Maria on backend readiness.
Got it. Holding the toggle. Will revisit after the load test lands.

subject: Weekly update — Finch App launch status (w/c 4 Apr)
summary: Ben's weekly written update to external stakeholder Lisa, summarising progress, blockers, and the pending launch-date reassessment. Lisa replies confirming she'll share with the beta cohort.

messages.bodyPreview: Hi Lisa — weekly update attached. Headlines: 4 of 15 actions closed, 3 blocked, auth fix is the critical path. Launch date officially at-risk pending the Apr 15 reassessment.
Thanks Ben — clear. I'll share the summary with the beta cohort and pre-warn them about a potential one-week slip. Let me know when the reassessment lands.

subject: #design — onboarding copy review
summary: Alex and James iterate on onboarding copy in Slack. James posts three variants for screen 2, Alex picks one and flags a length issue on mobile.

messages.bodyPreview: Three variants for screen 2 CTA — (a) 'Get started in 2 minutes' (b) 'Set up your first task' (c) 'Jump in'. Which reads best?
(b). It's specific and action-oriented. (a) wraps awkwardly on small iPhones — we measured 375px yesterday.
Going with (b). Pushing updated copy to staging in the next hour.

subject: Quick — call about auth bug?
summary: Short SMS exchange between Ben and Maria the night before the auth post-mortem. Ben pings Maria for a quick call to preview findings before the formal meeting.

messages.bodyPreview: Are you free to hop on a quick call before tomorrow's post-mortem? Want to make sure we're aligned on the root cause framing.
Yep. 10 min? Calling now.
